subject: [PATCH RFC v3 5/6] mm: use per-VMA lock in __access_remote_vm() for single-VMA accesses

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the __access_remote_vm() function by using a per-VMA lock instead of the global mmap_read_lock() when accessing memory within a single VMA. This reduces contention on large machines where tasks are reading /proc/PID/cmdline, /proc/PID/environ, or /proc/PID/mem. The patch also introduces a new access_remote_vma_ops() helper to handle special cases such as VM_IO/VM_PFNMAP VMAs and COWed pages. The approach taken is to use the per-VMA lock when possible, falling back to the global mmap_lock in case of VMA boundary crossings or other exceptions.
